puzzle.title: The Weight of Honesty
puzzle.intro: In a community defined by faith and sacrifice, a moment of decision arrived. For some, the comfort of the present outweighed the call to duty. This story is about a small group whose choice led not to outright rebellion, but to a heavy-hearted absence. Their subsequent path was one of profound isolation and a test of truthfulness, where the entire world seemed to shrink around them, waiting for a verdict from the heavens.
clues: We were devoted followers of the final Prophet (ﷺ) in the city of Madinah.
When the call came for a difficult and distant expedition, we stayed behind, not from disbelief, but from procrastination and attachment to our comforts.
While others made false excuses to the Prophet (ﷺ), we chose the difficult path of telling the complete truth about our failure.
As a consequence of our actions, a severe command was issued: the entire community was forbidden from speaking to us.
Our trial reached its peak when we were even commanded to separate from our spouses, leaving us utterly isolated.
After fifty days of sincere repentance, our forgiveness was announced directly from Allah in verses that are recited to this day in Surah At-Tawbah.

identity.label: Who Are We?
options: The Hypocrites who made false excuses
The Three Who Were Left Behind
identity.answer: The Three Who Were Left Behind

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Fleeing during the Battle of Uhud
Staying behind from the Expedition to Tabuk
Arguing over the spoils of the Battle of Badr
Refusing to pay Zakat after the Prophet's death
trial.answer: Staying behind from the Expedition to Tabuk

location.label: Key Element
options: Being swallowed by a large fish

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Receiving worldly leadership and a kingdom
Being exiled from the city of Madinah

puzzle.arabic: وَعَلَى ٱلثَّلَـٰثَةِ ٱلَّذِينَ خُلِّفُوا۟ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا ضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْأَرْضُ بِمَا رَحُبَتْ وَضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ أَنفُسُهُمْ وَظَنُّوٓا۟ أَن لَّا مَلْجَأَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَّآ إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ تَابَ عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَتُوبُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
puzzle.verse: And [He also forgave] the three who were left alone [i.e., boycotted, regretting their error] to the point that the earth closed in on them in spite of its vastness and their souls confined [i.e., anguished] them and they were certain that there is no refuge from Allāh except in Him. Then He turned to them so they could repent. Indeed, Allāh is the Accepting of Repentance, the Merciful. (9:118)
